zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Geoffrey le Scrope於1280年出生時，他的父親William le Scrope Lord of Bolton11277歲，母親Constance Newsom11277歲。他於大約1306年與Ivetta De Ros Scrope在英國越可爾馬斯哈姆結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1338年被列為english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench。他去世於1340年12月2日在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特享年60歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾可福哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Geoffrey le Scrope于1280年出生时，他的父亲William le Scrope Lord of Bolton11277岁，母亲Constance Newsom11277岁。他于大约1306年与Ivetta De Ros Scrope在英国越可爾馬斯哈姆结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1338年被列为english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench。他去世于1340年12月2日在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特享年60岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾可福哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Scrope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: of uncertain origin. Tengvik suggests derivation from the Old Scandinavian personal name Skrópi (of doubtful etymology, but compare Old Scandinavian skrópa-maðr ‘hypocrite’, containing the genitive plural form of skrópar ‘sham, hypocrisy’). It is possible, but not certain, that this personal name forms the first element of the Notts place-name Scrooby. Otherwise there is no evidence for its currency in England or, more relevantly, in Normandy. Richards Castle (in Herefs and Shrops) was apparently named after Richard Scrope, who held estates in Herefs and Worcs in 1066 and was a Norman favourite of Edward the Confessor. The Scrope family, whose name is traditionally pronounced /skru:p/, was politically important in medieval England, and acquired estates in Lincs (Barton on Humber), NR Yorks (Castle Bolton), and in ER Yorks (Flotmanby). Richard (le) Scrope of Bolton, in Wensleydale, was Lord Chancellor in the reign of Richard II. The use of le and de in the surname, suggesting either a nickname or a locative name, must be treated with caution because these were often added gratuitously to Anglo-Norman surnames as a sign of high social status.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Geoffrey le Scrope was born in 1280, his father, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, was 11277 and his mother, Constance Newsom, was 11277. He married Ivetta De Ros Scrope about 1306, in Masham, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. In 1338, at the age of 58, his occupation is listed as english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench. He died on 2 December 1340, in Gent, East Flanders, Belgium, at the age of 60, and was buried in Coverham, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Geoffrey le Scrope est né en 1280, son père, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, avait 11277 ans et sa mère, Constance Newsom, avait 11277 ans. Il a épousé Ivetta De Ros Scrope environ 1306, à Masham, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. En 1338, à l’âge de 58 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench. Il est décédé le 2 décembre 1340, à Gent, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Coverham, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Geoffrey le Scrope 1280 geboren wurde, war sein Vater William le Scrope Lord of Bolton 11277 und seine Mutter Constance Newsom 11277. Er hat um 1306 in Masham, Yorkshire, England Ivetta De Ros Scrope geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1338, als er 58 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench aufgeführt. Er starb am 2. Dezember 1340 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Gent, Ostflandern, Belgien und wurde in Coverham, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Geoffrey le Scrope è nato il nel 1280, il suo padre, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, aveva 11277 anni e il suo madre, Constance Newsom, aveva 11277 anni. Lui sposato Ivetta De Ros Scrope circa 1306, nel Masham, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Nel 1338, all'età di 58 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench. Morì il 2 dicembre 1340, nel Gent, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Coverham, North Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey le Scropeが1280年に生まれた時，父William le Scrope Lord of Boltonは11277歳，母Constance Newsomは11277歳であった。約1306年にイギリスヨークシュアイアマシャムでIvetta De Ros Scropeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。1338年，58歳の時，職業はenglish lawyer, and chief justice of the king's benchと記録されている。彼は1340年12月2日にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンジェントで60歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアコバーハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey le Scrope(이)가 1280년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William le Scrope Lord of Bolton(은)는 11277세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance Newsom(은)는 11277세였다. 그는 대략 1306년 영국 요르크실르 마샤에서 Ivetta De Ros Scrope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1338년, 58세의 나이로 그의 직업은 english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench이었다. 그는 1340년 12월 2일 벨기에 스트-블라아더 젠트에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 커버함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Geoffrey le Scrope nasceu em 1280, seu pai, William le Scrope Lord of Bolton, tinha 11277 anos e sua mãe, Constance Newsom, tinha 11277 anos. Ele casou-se com Ivetta De Ros Scrope aproximadamente 1306, em Masham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Em 1338, com 58 anos, sua profissão aparece como english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench. Ele faleceu em 2 de dezembro de 1340, em Gent, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Coverham, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Geoffrey le Scrope родился в 1280 г., его отцу по имени William le Scrope Lord of Bolton было 11277 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance Newsom, 11277. Он женился на женщине по имени Ivetta De Ros Scrope около 1306 г. в местности машам, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. В 1338 году, в возрасте 58 лет,  он работал по профессии english lawyer, and chief justice of the king's bench. Он умер 2 декабря 1340 г. в местности джент, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности ковирам, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
